assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a Version Impact Table (lines 9-15) showing 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked YES (h2 0.4.5, < 0.4.8) and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) marked NO (h2 >= 0.4.8). The table references specific build tags and lock file h2 versions as evidence.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md line 10 shows Proposed versions as 'RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1' (only 2.1.x). Line 17 explicitly states 'Remove RHTPA 2.2.0' and line 22 confirms 'No 2.2.x versions are affected'. The Jira update on lines 26-30 sets versions to only RHTPA 2.1.0 and RHTPA 2.1.1.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 7 states 'The 2.1.x stream is affected' and 'The 2.2.x stream is NOT affected'. Line 13 states 'No remediation is needed for the 2.2.x stream -- it already ships the fixed version.' Two remediation tasks are created: Task 1 targets 'release/0.3.z' (2.1.x upstream, line 19/30) and Task 2 targets 'rhtpa-release.0.3.z' (2.1.x downstream, line 69/79). No tasks reference the 2.2.x stream.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 23 states 'Unscoped (no stream suffix in summary)'. Lines 37 explicitly state: 'This issue is treated as unscoped -- it covers all configured streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x). Steps 2-7 will analyze all streams and scope remediation to actually affected streams only.' The version-impact.md table includes versions from both 2.1.x (2.1.0, 2.1.1) and 2.2.x (2.2.0-2.2.4) streams, confirming all streams were analyzed.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: No cross-stream impact notice appears anywhere in the output files. The remediation.md file goes directly from version impact analysis to Case A remediation task creation without any cross-stream impact notice. The issue is treated as unscoped throughout (data-extraction.md line 23, affects-versions.md line 14 'unscoped issue -- all streams analyzed'), meaning Case B (which applies only to scoped issues) is correctly not triggered.
